name: POLICY-BACKLOG-15
title: Cross-cutting CLAUDE.md @-import tests belong to a different scope
description:
Wave 7 audit observed two test files under .claude/features/policy/test/ that walk the entire repo for CLAUDE.md files and verify @-import behaviour:

(1) test-imports-resolve.py — asserts every @-import in every CLAUDE.md across the repo resolves to an existing file.
(2) test-no-stale-imports.py — asserts no CLAUDE.md @-imports the retired .claude/features/policy/workflow-rules.md, and uses test-imports-resolve.py as a regex-correctness probe.

These checks are about CLAUDE.md generation hygiene (a rabbit-cage concern — rabbit-cage owns install.py and the generate-claude-md producer that emits CLAUDE.md). Only the second test traces tangentially to policy Inv 2 (workflow-rules.md absent); the first traces to no policy invariant at all.

Per the housekeeping protocol's audit criterion 'Spec-only coverage' and 'Bounded Scope' philosophy: candidate to relocate both tests to rabbit-cage (or a future claude-md-lint feature) and shrink policy/test/ to invariant-conforming coverage only. No urgency — defer until a future audit wave touches rabbit-cage's test surface, since the change crosses scopes.

history.note: Wave 8 audit — fixed via cross-feature touch. CLAUDE.md @-import tests moved from policy/test/ to rabbit-cage/test/ as test-claude-md-imports-resolve.py and test-claude-md-no-stale-imports.py; rabbit-cage spec Inv 14 codifies ownership.
